User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Catalyst Catalog application, allowing for personalized experiences like wishlists and carts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication. Assumes an external authentication system handles passwords.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for products, enabling organized browsing and filtering.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Clothing').

description.description: A brief description of the category, providing context or examples of products it contains.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an individual product available in the Catalyst Catalog for display, search, and purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, including features and specifications.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

imageUrl.description: The URL to the main image of the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was first added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product details were last updated.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a shopping cart belonging to a user, holding items selected for purchase. Each user is assumed to have one active cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this cart. (Relationship: User 1:1 Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart was last modified (e.g., items added/removed, quantity changed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a specific product and its quantity within a shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product included in this cart item. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart.

priceAtAddition.description: The price of the product at the moment it was added to the cart, useful for order history or price change tracking.

Wishlist.title: Wishlist
Wishlist.description: Represents a user's collection of desired products for future reference or purchase. Each user is assumed to have one wishlist.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Wishlist entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this wishlist. (Relationship: User 1:1 Wishlist)

name.description: An optional name for the wishlist (e.g., 'My Favorites', 'Birthday List').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the wishlist was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the wishlist was last modified (e.g., items added/removed).

WishlistItem.title: WishlistItem
WishlistItem.description: Represents a specific product included in a user's wishlist.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WishlistItem entity.

wishlistId.description: Reference to the Wishlist this item belongs to. (Relationship: Wishlist 1:N WishlistItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product included in this wishlist item. (Relationship: Product 1:N WishlistItem)

addedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was added to the wishlist.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, including login details and personal data. Each document is owned by the authenticated user matching `{userId}`. Security rules will ensure `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection of product categories. Read-only for all users, enabling filtering of products. Admin users would have write access.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product category.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection of product details. Includes `categoryId` for filtering and `price` for sorting. Read-only for all users, enabling browsing and search. Admin users would have write access.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores a user's shopping cart. Designed for a 1:1 relationship where `cartId` must match `userId`. Each document is owned by the user matching `{userId}`. The document itself also stores `userId` for authorization independence. Security rules will enforce that `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid` and `{cartId}` matches `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the cart, which must match `userId` for a 1:1 relationship.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a user's shopping cart. Each document includes denormalized `userId` and `cartId` fields for authorization independence, allowing security rules to directly verify ownership without `get()` calls. Security rules will ensure `resource.data.userId` matches `request.auth.uid` and `userId` (path wildcard), and `resource.data.cartId` matches `cartId` (path wildcard).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent cart, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent cart, which must match `userId`.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the cart item.

definition.description: Stores a user's wishlist. Designed for a 1:1 relationship where `wishlistId` must match `userId`. Each document is owned by the user matching `{userId}`. The document itself also stores `userId` for authorization independence. Security rules will enforce that `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid` and `{wishlistId}` matches `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this wishlist, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: wishlistId
params.description: The unique identifier of the wishlist, which must match `userId` for a 1:1 relationship.

definition.description: Stores individual products within a user's wishlist. Each document includes denormalized `userId` and `wishlistId` fields for authorization independence, allowing security rules to directly verify ownership without `get()` calls. Security rules will ensure `resource.data.userId` matches `request.auth.uid` and `userId` (path wildcard), and `resource.data.wishlistId` matches `wishlistId` (path wildcard).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent wishlist, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: wishlistId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent wishlist, which must match `userId`.

params.name: wishlistItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the wishlist item.

definition.description: Designates a user as an administrator. The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{userId}` grants admin privileges to the specified user `userId`. This follows the DBAC pattern (Database-Backed Access Control) and 'existence over content' principle. Read access to this collection is restricted to authenticated users to check their own admin status, or by other admins to manage roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user designated as an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Catalyst Catalog is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. It strategically uses structural segregation and denormalization to ensure robust security rules and efficient queries.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved primarily through denormalization. For user-specific subcollections (e.g., CartItems, WishlistItems), critical authorization context such as `userId` and `cartId`/`wishlistId` is denormalized and stored directly within each subcollection document. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore security rules to check parent document ownership. For example, a `CartItem` document at `/users/{userId}/carts/{cartId}/items/{cartItemId}` will explicitly contain `resource.data.userId` and `resource.data.cartId` fields, allowing rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and `resource.data.cartId == userId` (where `userId` is the path wildcard).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure ensures that `list` operations are secure and predictable. Publicly accessible data, such as `Categories` and `Products`, resides in top-level collections (`/categories`, `/products`). These collections have a homogeneous security posture (read-only for all authenticated/unauthenticated users), meaning `list` queries will return all visible data without needing complex filtering logic in rules. User-specific private data (profiles, carts, wishlists) is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}` paths. This path-based ownership naturally limits `list` operations to the authenticated user's own data (`request.auth.uid == userId`), preventing unauthorized access to other users' private information.

**Structural Segregation:** Data with different security requirements are placed in separate collections. Public product and category information is distinct from private user data. Within user data, each user's profile, cart, and wishlist are isolated within their `/users/{userId}` subtrees, preventing accidental cross-user data exposure. The 1:1 relationships for `Cart` and `Wishlist` per user are implemented by using the `userId` as the document ID within dedicated subcollections (`/users/{userId}/carts/{userId}`, `/users/{userId}/wishlists/{userId}`), enforcing strict ownership and a clear hierarchy.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** `User` profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`, secured by path-based ownership (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
*   **User-Owned 1:N/1:1 Data:** `Cart` and `Wishlist` entities follow the `/users/{userId}/<entity-plural>/{entityId}` pattern, where for 1:1 relationships, `{entityId}` is set to `{userId}`. This makes ownership explicit and rules simple.
*   **Nested 1:N Data:** `CartItem`s and `WishlistItem`s are nested further, e.g., `/users/{userId}/carts/{cartId}/items/{cartItemId}`, inheriting security from their parent paths and leveraging denormalized fields.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** An `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection is included for future admin functionality, using the 'existence over content' principle to grant permissions without custom claims.

**Data Clarity:** Naming conventions are consistent and descriptive (e.g., `productId`, `categoryId`). A dedicated `price` field in `Product` enables the requested sorting functionality.

This design prioritizes ease of writing, understanding, and debugging Firestore security rules while maintaining scalability and performance.
